lines.target: Ungood will do just as well — because it's an exact opposite, other is not.
lines.base: Nobueno haría también la misma función — mejor debido a que es exactamente lo contrario, lo cual no es el caso de la otra.

lines.target: Or again, if you want a stronger word for good, what use is there in having a whole string of vague useless words like «excellent» and splendid and all the rest?
lines.base: O entonces, Si usted desea una versión más fuerte de bueno, ¿de qué sirve tener una sarta completa de palabras vagas e inútiles como excelente y espléndido y todas las demás?

lines.target: Plusgood covers the meaning; or doubleplusgood if you want something stronger still.
lines.base: Plusbueno cubre ese sentido; o dobleplusbueno si usted desea algo todavía más fuerte. (...) Finalmente, toda la idea del bien y del mal

lines.target: In the end, the whole notion of goodness and badness will be covered by only six words — in reality one word.
lines.base: será cubierta por seis palabras solamente — en realidad una sola palabra. ¿No ve usted la belleza de eso, Winston?

lines.target: Don't you see the beauty of that, Winston?
lines.base: Usted no es consciente de la belleza de la destrucción de palabras. ¿Sabe usted que «Newspeak» es el único lenguaje del mundo cuyo vocabulario se restringe todos los años?

lines.target: You don't grasp the beauty of the destruction of words. Do you know that Newspeak is the only language in the world whose vocabulary gets smaller every year?
lines.base: Al final, terminaremos haciendo literalmente imposible el crimen del pensamiento, debido a que no existirán palabras para expresarlo.

lines.target: Finally, we shall make thoughtcrime literally impossible, because there will be no words in which to express it.

gap_candidates.target: Ungood will do just as well — because it's an exact opposite, other is not.
Or again, if you want a stronger word for good, what use is there in having a whole string of vague useless words like «excellent» and splendid and all the rest?
Plusgood covers the meaning; or doubleplusgood if you want something stronger still.
In the end, the whole notion of goodness and badness will be covered by only six words — in reality one word.
Don't you see the beauty of that, Winston?
You don't grasp the beauty of the destruction of words. Do you know that Newspeak is the only language in the world whose vocabulary gets smaller every year?
Finally, we shall make thoughtcrime literally impossible, because there will be no words in which to express it.
